sections.heading: What Work-Life Balance Actually Means
sections.body: Work-life balance isn't a 50/50 split between work and personal time. It's a sustainable rhythm where neither work nor personal life consistently dominates. For developers, the biggest threat is the 'always-on' culture: remote work blurring boundaries, Slack on your phone, and the guilt of 'not doing enough.' True balance means you can fully engage at work AND fully disengage afterward.

sections.heading: Setting Boundaries That Stick
sections.body: Boundaries aren't selfish — they're professional. Here are the specific boundaries developers need:

code.snippet:
DEVELOPER BOUNDARY TOOLKIT:

TIME BOUNDARIES:
• Fixed work hours (e.g., 9 AM - 5:30 PM)
• No Slack/email after shutdown ritual
• Calendar blocks for personal time (exercise, family)
• 'No meeting' mornings for deep work

COMMUNICATION BOUNDARIES:
• Slack status: 'Offline after 5 PM, will respond at 9 AM'
• Email auto-response after hours
• Phone on Do Not Disturb during personal time
• On-call boundaries (max 1 week/month, with compensation)

PSYCHOLOGICAL BOUNDARIES:
• Don't check 'just one thing' after hours
• Don't take work guilt into personal time
• Don't take personal stress into work time
• Shutdown ritual creates the mental boundary

SOCIAL BOUNDARIES:
• Don't apologize for having a life
• Don't let others' hustle guilt affect you
• Say no to non-essential commitments
• Protect weekend time fiercely
code.explanation: Boundaries require communication AND consistency. Tell your team your hours, then actually stick to them. The first few weeks are hardest — after that, people respect your boundaries.

sections.heading: The Disconnection Protocol
sections.body: Disconnecting from work isn't automatic — you need a protocol. The shutdown ritual (from the Habits module) is your primary tool. Additional strategies: change your physical environment (move to a different room), change your clothes (remove work attire), and engage in a completely different activity (cooking, exercise, gaming). The goal: signal to your brain that work is OVER.

jargon.definition: A sustainable rhythm where neither work nor personal life consistently dominates. It's not 50/50 — it's what's sustainable for YOU.
A clear line between work and personal time. Requires communication AND consistency to maintain.
A consistent end-of-work routine that signals to your brain that work is done, enabling mental disconnection.
The expectation that developers are available 24/7 via Slack, email, and on-call. The primary threat to work-life balance in tech.

summary: Work-life balance is a sustainable rhythm, not a 50/50 split. Set time, communication, and psychological boundaries. Use the shutdown ritual to disconnect. The biggest threat is always-on culture — combat it with fixed work hours, Do Not Disturb, and protecting personal time fiercely.
